title: The Bone Temple Continues A Franchise Trend And Fixes A Major Problem
text: The ScreenRant article examines how 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple exemplifies successful franchise revival in the horror genre. Horror had by far the best movie genre of 2025 largely due to franchises being revitalized with creative excellence. Both Final Destination: Bloodlines and the 28 Years Later sequels demonstrate that even sequels can break the mold of uncreative studio endeavors. Director Nia DaCosta's film solves a significant issue introduced in 28 Years Later (2025). The original sequel had made the infected more physically threatening through the Alpha infected, which rendered the established cure storyline from 28 Weeks Later obsolete. The Bone Temple creatively restores narrative possibility by humanizing the Alpha infected character Samson through opiate treatment. This approach not only develops compelling character arcs but also reestablishes the possibility of an actual cure, reviving abandoned plot threads while maintaining fresh storytelling. The film succeeds by focusing on human characters and moral complexity within the post-apocalyptic setting rather than relying solely on zombie action sequences.
